tests/fixtures/material_ssh_fixture*.openssh-v1 — reproduction notes
====================================================================

These files are OpenSSH v1 ASCII-armored private-key fixtures, generated
once and committed verbatim. We do NOT regenerate at CI time because
key generation uses OS randomness — the same recipe produces a different
key each run; only the format + algorithm + comment ("") are deterministic.

Files
-----

  material_ssh_fixture.openssh-v1          Ed25519, empty comment, no passphrase
                                            (387 B; happy-path / canonical-bytes /
                                             banner / round-trip + wire-budget tests)
  material_ssh_fixture_rsa1024.openssh-v1  RSA 1024-bit, empty comment, no passphrase
                                            (1020 B; for the RSA<2048 [DEPRECATED] tag
                                             test in tests/ssh_banner_render.rs)

  (DSA fixture: NOT committed. See "DSA fixture status" below.)

Material::SshKey JCS envelope fixture
  material_ssh_signable.bin                JCS bytes of an Envelope{SshKey{bytes:
                                            CANONICAL_RE_ENCODED_ED25519}} — pins
                                            byte-identity across runs (SSH-07).
                                            Note: bytes stored in Material::SshKey
                                            are the CANONICAL re-encoded form per
                                            D-P7-11 (ssh-key::PrivateKey::to_openssh
                                            (LineEnding::LF)). To regenerate, run
                                            `cargo test --test material_ssh_envelope_round_trip
                                            -- --ignored regenerate_material_ssh_envelope_fixture`.

The fixture is used for:
  1. Empirical byte-determinism guard test
     (src/payload/ingest.rs::ssh_tests::ssh_key_canonical_re_encode_is_byte_deterministic)
     — validates D-P7-11 on ssh-key 0.6.7's PrivateKey::from_openssh +
     to_openssh(LineEnding::LF) round-trip.
  2. Phase 7 Plan 08 round-trip / banner-render / ingest / envelope tests
     reference the same fixture files.

Recipes (require OpenSSH client tools)
--------------------------------------

  ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "" -N "" -f /tmp/cipherpost_ssh_fixture_key
  cp /tmp/cipherpost_ssh_fixture_key tests/fixtures/material_ssh_fixture.openssh-v1

  ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 1024 -C "" -N "" -f /tmp/cipherpost_ssh_rsa1024_fixture
  cp /tmp/cipherpost_ssh_rsa1024_fixture tests/fixtures/material_ssh_fixture_rsa1024.openssh-v1

The keys are unencrypted (-N "" → empty passphrase). Cipherpost's ingest
pipeline NEVER asks the user to encrypt material — it accepts whatever
OpenSSH v1 PrivateKey blob the user supplies, then encrypts the WHOLE
envelope under age (X25519, recipient-targeted). Encrypted-at-rest SSH
keys are out of scope for this milestone (D-P7-12 user-facing message
points users at `ssh-keygen -p -o -f <path>` for format conversion ONLY,
not encryption removal).

DSA fixture status (Plan 08 Task 1 deferral)
--------------------------------------------

Modern OpenSSH (system ssh-keygen) refuses DSA generation:

  $ ssh-keygen -t dsa -C "" -N "" -f /tmp/dsa
  unknown key type dsa

Generation alternatives considered:
  (a) Older-OpenSSH host (8.x) — not available in the build environment.
  (b) ssh-key crate's KeyGen API in an examples/ binary — requires turning on
      the `dsa` and `rand_core` features on ssh-key, which would expand the
      supply-chain surface (pulling the `dsa` crate). D-P7-10's verified-clean
      ssh-key shape uses `default-features = false, features = ["alloc"]`;
      adding DSA generation features just for one fixture violates that
      invariant.
  (c) Curated DSA fixture from an external test-data repo — unaudited;
      rejected for the same supply-chain hygiene reason.

Decision: SKIP the DSA fixture for Plan 08 (per the plan's <action> Step A
fallback option 3). The RSA-1024 fixture covers the [DEPRECATED] tag
golden-string assertion (RSA<2048 case). DSA-deprecation rendering is
covered indirectly by:
  - src/preview.rs::tests::is_deprecated_ssh_algorithm_dsa_always_deprecated
    (Plan 06 unit test pinning the predicate logic — DSA at any size is
    flagged deprecated).
  - tests/ssh_banner_render.rs::render_ssh_preview_dsa_carries_deprecated_tag
    is OMITTED from this plan's test file. SUMMARY.md records the rationale.

Wire-budget posture (D-P7-03 fallback active per research GAP for SSH)
----------------------------------------------------------------------

  Minimum Ed25519 OpenSSH v1 PEM:  387 B (measured; raw fixture file)
  Predicted encoded packet (4.16x): ~1610 B
  PKARR BEP44 ceiling:              1000 B
  Verdict: SSH does NOT round-trip at any size today.
  tests/ssh_roundtrip.rs's `ssh_self_round_trip_recovers_canonical_bytes`
  is `#[ignore]`'d FROM DAY 1 with the EXACT note text per D-P7-03:
    "wire-budget: minimum OpenSSH v1 Ed25519 blob exceeds 1000-byte
     BEP44 ceiling (~1340 B encoded) — see Pitfall #22 / v1.2 milestone"

  RSA-1024 fixture (1020 B raw) is even larger — used ONLY as a
  banner-render fixture (preview path, no wire transit involved).

SHA-256 of committed files
--------------------------

  3cb6b44426bd6c004348a65d8c42b694e7256df6bbd3d361528c59025acf1e46  material_ssh_fixture.openssh-v1
  57db1cd9b49fe9b58a0d721a01a498b114a1be71dfb3ecbf969d69c0caf280ed  material_ssh_fixture_rsa1024.openssh-v1

  (DSA fixture: not committed)
  (material_ssh_signable.bin: regenerate via `cargo test --test
   material_ssh_envelope_round_trip -- --ignored
   regenerate_material_ssh_envelope_fixture`; SHA pinned by the byte-identity
   test in that file.)

Public-key reference (Ed25519 fixture)
--------------------------------------

  ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIJFHrl4QVZLni+CoonIHDhIGt+ayrVNH7YjeAidxtlJE

Generated: Ed25519 + reproduction note authored 2026-04-24 (Plan 05);
RSA-1024 + DSA-skip note added 2026-04-25 (Plan 08). System OpenSSH client.
